name: Tooling Worth Stealing Lens
purpose: Find concrete product, UX, CLI, or systems ideas that are worth borrowing, adapting, or reinterpreting in your own tools.
questions: What implementation pattern is actually worth stealing?
Is the idea specific enough to reproduce?
Would it create leverage in current tools or workflows?
Can it be adapted rather than copied literally?
Does it improve UX, developer experience, or system capability?
ignoreRules: Brand positioning with no concrete mechanism
Ideas that depend entirely on inaccessible proprietary infrastructure
Features that are obvious but not differentiated
High-level claims with no visible implementation pattern
